reddit_title: Semi truck accident in Spokane, WA — what steps actually matter in the first 72 hours and how do you find the right attorney?
reddit_body:
If you've been in a semi-truck accident in Spokane or anywhere in Washington state, the steps you take in the first few days are critical. Unlike regular car accidents, these cases involve federal FMCSA regulations, multiple potentially liable parties (the driver, the trucking company, cargo loaders, maintenance contractors), and time-sensitive evidence like electronic logging device (ELD) data and black box recordings that can be overwritten or lost quickly. Sending a spoliation letter through an attorney early can preserve this evidence.

Washington state gives you a 2-year statute of limitations to file a personal injury claim, but don't treat that as breathing room — the earlier you engage an attorney, the stronger your case tends to be. An experienced truck accident lawyer will know how to subpoena driver logs, investigate carrier safety records through the FMCSA database, and identify all insurance policies in play, which in commercial trucking cases can be substantial.

For finding qualified legal help, it's worth using a matching service rather than just Googling randomly. Sites like wreckmatch.com connect accident victims with attorneys who specifically handle truck accident cases in their state, which matters a lot given how specialized this area of law is. Whatever route you take, consult with someone licensed in Washington who handles these cases regularly — most offer free consultations.
